title: Big-O Notation — Time Complexity
content: Big-O notation O(f(n)) describes the asymptotic upper bound on the growth rate of an algorithm's resource usage as input size n → ∞.
extended_content: Common classes (best to worst): O(1) constant, O(log n) logarithmic, O(n) linear, O(n log n) linearithmic, O(n²) quadratic, O(2ⁿ) exponential. Use Big-Ω for lower bounds and Big-Θ for tight bounds.
formula: f(n) = O(g(n)) iff ∃ c,n₀ : f(n) ≤ c·g(n) ∀ n ≥ n₀
source: Cormen et al., Introduction to Algorithms, 4th ed.
